EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical Storm Andrea crossed the forecast area from the Gulf of Mexico. SE Ga and NE Fl were on the northeast side of the storm...which focused onshore convergent bands. A few tornadoes were spawned along the Duval and Nassau county Atlantic coasts...with gusty tropical storm force winds and locally heavy rainfall over inland areas. Twenty-four hour rainfall amounts ranged from 1 to 4 inches...with the highest value of 3.62 inches 4 ESE of Waycross. Rainfall amounts decreased to the SE with an observation site 5 NNW of Spuds only reporting 0.06 inches. Wind gusts of 30 to 40 mph were common along the coast and estuary systems. The highest gust over northeast Fl was 38 knots measured at Naval Air Station Jacksonville from the south on the 6th at 11:06 pm. The highest gust over southeast Georgia was 39 knots measured from the WNW at 6:28 am at the St. Simons Airport. The highest wind gust along the coast was reported from a WeatherFlow (mesonet) station located at the Jacksonville Beach Pier with a speed of 72 knots reported at 4:37 pm on the 6th. This gust was likely caused by a passing waterspout that moved onshore near Mayport. The lowest pressure measured was from the Jekyll Island WeatherFlow (mesonet) station of 993.0 mb on the 7th at 12:30 am. The highest wind gust measured over the adjacent Atlantic waters was 39 kts at buoy 41012 43 NM east of St. Augustine on the 6th at 10:50 pm. The C-Man station SAUF1 in St. Augustine measured a gust to 42 knots on the 6th at 7:58 pm.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At 12:25 pm, trees were blown down and blocked NE 12th Street near 31st Avenue in Gainesville. At 12:35 pm, a road was closed due to flooding at SW Depot Ave in the 700 block, located in Gainesville. The highest rainfall amount in Alachua was measured by a CoCoRaHS observer located 7 miles W of Micanopy which was 1.87 inches. The highest wind gust measured by the Gainesville Regional Airport ASOS was 28 knots from the SE on the 6th at 3:28 pm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong thunderstorm associated with diurnal convection produced nickel sized hail in southern Lake County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A weather spotter observed nickel sized hail falling near Lake Louisa.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered strong diurnal convection become locally severe as a storm produced large hail between Highway 520 and Highway 528 in rural eastern Orange County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A report courtesy of the NSSL SHAVE Project indicated quarter size hail in the town of Christmas within rural eastern Orange County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered strong diurnal convection become locally severe as a storm produced large hail between Highway 520 and Highway 528 in rural eastern Orange County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A report courtesy of the NSSL SHAVE Project confirmed ping-pong ball sized hail fell in rural eastern Orange County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Indian River County Beach Patrol confirmed media reports of a drowning. The main died after being caught in a strong rip current at a beach park in Vero Beach.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 22 year old male visiting from Hawaii was caught in a strong rip current and drowned just offshore the South Beach Park in Vero Beach during the early evening.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Localized heavy rainfall associated with an area of slow moving thunderstorms produced between four and seven inches of rain in only a few hours across |western Volusia County. Temporary street flooding, wth water entering one home, were the major impacts. The flooding quickly receded once the heavy rain |ended.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A Weather Underground mesonet site at Lake Helen recorded 6.47 inches of rain in less than 3 hours due to slow moving thunderstorms.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous reports of a funnel cloud were received in association with a boundary moving southwest of a line of convection which was advancing toward the coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement received multiple reports of a funnel cloud to the northwest of Lakewood Park, to the southwest of a line of convection.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home in Palm City. Significant damage resulted from the strike and subsquent fire.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A home sustained damage estimated at $500,000 due to a fire which started after the home was struck by lightning.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A trough over the eastern United States combined with deep tropical moisture over the region with a slow moving front sagging southward over Georgia combined with the sea breezes to produce a very active convective day. Thunderstorm activity drifted inland from the SW and Gulf Coast region...with activity further blossoming toward the Atlantic coast and over SE Ga during the afternoon and evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An observer measured 1.8 inches of rainfall within 45 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front passed over the Florida peninsula on February 16th with cool air filling in behind the front.  Temperatures fell down to near freezing along the Nature Coast on the morning of the 17th, but the coldest temperatures were recorded on the morning of the 18th.||Temperatures stayed below freezing for up to 8 hours over the Nature Coast with hard freeze conditions occurring for up to 6 hours.  Over Central Florida, freezing temperatures were recorded for around 2 to 4 hours with isolated locations recording a few hours of hard freeze conditions.  Parts of southwest Florida experienced 2 to 3 hours of sub-freezing temperatures. ||No significant crop damage was reported.||Levy County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 6 to 8 hours across interior portions of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 25 degrees at the RAWS site at Lower Suwannee.||Citrus County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 6 to 8 hours across interior portions of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 27 degrees at the AWOS in Inverness.||Hernando County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 8 hours over interior portions of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 27 degrees at the mesonet site 2 miles northwest of Weeki Wachee.||Sumter County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for 4 to 6 hours across much of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 25 degrees at the FAWN site at Okahumpka, near the eastern Sumter county border.||Pasco County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 6 hours across much of the interior portions of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 27 degrees at the mesonet site 1 mile east-northeast of Zepherhills.||Isolated spots in interior Hillsborough County recorded freezing temperatures for around 2 hours.  The coldest temperature reported was 32 degrees at the AWOS at Tampa Executive Airport.||Isolated spots in Polk County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 2 hours hours.  The coldest temperature reported was 30 degrees at the AWOS in Bartow.||Isolated spots in interior Manatee County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for up to 2 hours.  The coldest temperature reported was 30 degrees at the mesonet site 4 miles west-northwest of Lake Manatee State Park.| |Isolated spots in interior Sarasota County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 3 hours.  The coldest temperature reported was 30 degrees at the FAWN site at North Port.||Hardee County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for up to 4 hours across much of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 28 degrees at the COOP site at Wauchula.||Isolated spots in Highlands County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for up to 4 hours.  The coldest temperature reported was 28 degrees at the COOP site at Archbold.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Citrus County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 6 to 8 hours across interior portions of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 27 degrees at the AWOS in Inverness.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level trough of low pressure moved across South Florida producing scattered strong to severe thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An off duty NWS employee reported penny-sized hail near the intersection of Palmetto Expressway and Okeechobee Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure over the southeastern states resulted in breezy onshore winds along the southeast coast of the Florida peninsula with strong rip currents.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A woman was taken to Jupiter Medical Center after a near-drowning at Dubois Park in Jupiter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A plume of deep tropical moisture combined with passing upper level disturbances sparks locally heavy rainfall producing cells.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A daily rainfall total of 2.05 inches was reported on Fleming Island.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A plume of deep tropical moisture combined with passing upper level disturbances sparks locally heavy rainfall producing cells.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A daily rainfall total of 3 inches was measured through 4 pm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High moisture content and active SW flow aloft continue to fueled numerous showers and thunderstorms across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter reported funnel clouds south of Bunnell.

Parts of southwest Florida experienced 2 to 3 hours of sub-freezing temperatures. ||No significant crop damage was reported.||Levy County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 6 to 8 hours across interior portions of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 25 degrees at the RAWS site at Lower Suwannee.||Citrus County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 6 to 8 hours across interior portions of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 27 degrees at the AWOS in Inverness.||Hernando County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 8 hours over interior portions of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 27 degrees at the mesonet site 2 miles northwest of Weeki Wachee.||Sumter County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for 4 to 6 hours across much of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 25 degrees at the FAWN site at Okahumpka, near the eastern Sumter county border.||Pasco County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 6 hours across much of the interior portions of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 27 degrees at the mesonet site 1 mile east-northeast of Zepherhills.||Isolated spots in interior Hillsborough County recorded freezing temperatures for around 2 hours.  The coldest temperature reported was 32 degrees at the AWOS at Tampa Executive Airport.||Isolated spots in Polk County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 2 hours hours.  The coldest temperature reported was 30 degrees at the AWOS in Bartow.||Isolated spots in interior Manatee County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for up to 2 hours.  The coldest temperature reported was 30 degrees at the mesonet site 4 miles west-northwest of Lake Manatee State Park.| |Isolated spots in interior Sarasota County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for around 3 hours.  The coldest temperature reported was 30 degrees at the FAWN site at North Port.||Hardee County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for up to 4 hours across much of the county.  The coldest temperature reported was 28 degrees at the COOP site at Wauchula.||Isolated spots in Highlands County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for up to 4 hours.  The coldest temperature reported was 28 degrees at the COOP site at Archbold.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Isolated spots in Highlands County recorded sub-freezing temperatures for up to 4 hours.  The coldest temperature reported was 28 degrees at the COOP site at Archbold.

One resident near the intersection of 13th Street and West Ave. reported water flooded his music room and the front yard of his house. Neighbors also spent the afternoon drying up the entrances to their apartments. There was no other serious reports of damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level TUTT retrograde over south Florida with dry air over NE Florida enhancing downdrafts in scattered afternoon thunderstorms...which focused inland due to onshore flow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A victim was struck near 74th Terrace and County Road 795 on the north side of Live Oak. Minor injuries were reported and the victim was transported to the hospital.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Sea breeze and outflow mergers produced very heavy rainfall that extended several hours over Hamilton county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Four hour rainfall total was 5.5 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Prevailing SW flowed focused numerous evening thunderstorm activity toward the St. Johns River Basin. Large scale cyclonic flow helped spawn a few strong to severe storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees and power line wires were blown down around the 5500 block of 118th Street. The time of the event was based on radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Prevailing SW flowed focused numerous evening thunderstorm activity toward the St. Johns River Basin. Large scale cyclonic flow helped spawn a few strong to severe storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees were blown down on 4 homes along 118th Street on the Jacksonville Westside, and one tree was blown down on a car. No injuries were reported. The time of the event was based on radar imagery.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A trough over the eastern United States combined with deep tropical moisture over the region with a slow moving front sagging southward over Georgia combined with the sea breezes to produce a very active convective day. Thunderstorm activity drifted inland from the SW and Gulf Coast region...with activity further blossoming toward the Atlantic coast and over SE Ga during the afternoon and evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two funnel clouds were observed by deputies near Highway 328.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface front lingered over the region and combined with high moisture from the remnants of TS Chantal over NE Florida and diurnal heating, numerous thunderstorms were spawned during the afternoon which produced locally heavy rainfall and gusty winds across northeast Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: McCoys Creek was flooded and the road partially closed. The cost of damage was estimated for inclusion of the event in Storm Data.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another round of major flash flooding occurred across parts of northwest Florida during the middle of August as an upper level trough developed west of the area with a continuous moisture feed off of the Gulf of Mexico streaming northward into the area.  This flooding hit many of the same areas impacted by the July flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Due to recent heavy rainfall and increased water flow on Bruce Creek, the bridge at Indian Creek Ranch Road has been displaced.  Monetary damage was estimated.

This flooding hit many of the same areas impacted by the July flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road closed due to high water near the intersection of Highway 388 and 77.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall during the month led to areal flooding over the southwest portion of the town of Mayo.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water levels started rising on the SW side of Mayo out of a drainage area and began to inundate a portion of the town sometime late on Aug 21st or early in the 22nd.  Rainfall from the 22nd and early on the 23rd worsened the problem.  The area impacted was generally bounded between MLK Blvd and Roosevelt Circle. There were 6 roads in the area deemed impassible and many others flooded. These were a combination of dirt roads and paved roads. Some mobile homes had shallow water surrounding them but not entering the structures.  A pump was used to reduce water levels in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A 45 year old male from Mississippi drowned off of Cape San Blas, likely due to rip currents.
Thunderstorms produced prolific lightning which caused a fire in northwest Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused house fire and a shed fire due to separate lightning strikes near East Milton.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: An observer measured 2.3 inches of rainfall within 2 hours.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was blown down near the intersection of Highway 79 and Millers Ferry Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two lightning strikes produced a small fire and damaged appliances inside a high-rise condominium in the Coco Plum section of Marathon, Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two lightning strikes occurred to the Bonefish Towers complex in the Coco Plum section of the city of Marathon.  The first bolt was observed to strike a small grouping of palm trees, which were set on fire.  The local fire department extinguished the fire.  A second strike to the main rooftop dryer vent melted the aluminum framing around dryer lint screens on the 4th, 7th, 8th and 9th floors along the southeast corner of the condominum tower.  Laundry rooms exist on all floors of the condominium except the 14th floor penthouse.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A ridge axis north of Florida brought southeast flow to the area with deep moisture and sea breeze convergence over the western half of the Florida peninsula. Afternoon and early evening thunderstorms produced damaging wind gusts in the Tampa Bay area and southern Nature Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media relayed a picture of a tree knocked down on a house in Tampa.  Time was estimated from radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep moisture and easterly flow supported afternoon sea breeze thunderstorms over the western half of Florida. One of these storms produced strong wind gusts that knocked down trees in the Tampa Bay area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported 2-3 downed oak trees along Bloomingdale Avenue. Time was estimated from radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep tropical moisture fueled thunderstorms in the Tampa Bay area when the east and west coast sea breezes collided overhead. One of these storms knocked down power lines and a carport along Gandy Boulevard.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An NWS employee reported strong winds knocked over power lines and damaged a car port along Gandy Boulevard in Pinellas County, associated with a down burst signature on radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a house in Tallahassee and resulted in a fire.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a house on the 200 block of Timberlane Dr and resulted in a fire.  The damage was estimated at $100,000.  This was reported by the City of Tallahassee Fire Department facebook page.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A southwest coast sea breeze moved inland during the late afternoon with a few showers developing along the boundary.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported a funnel cloud over Lake Trafford west of Immokalee.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Sea breeze storms under prevailing southwest flow fired during the afternoon and evening hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was reported over the Intracoastal Waterway.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Key West Police Department officers reported the 100 to 300 blocks of Duval Street were barricaded due to street flooding.  Public reports were received of flooding within one block of Front and Duval Streets.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm with heavy rainfall produced significant street flooding in the Old Town Historical District of Key West, as well as along the north side of the New Town section.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Key West Police Department officers reported the left lane of North Roosevelt Boulevard was closed within a few blocks of 17th Street due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cluster of slow moving thunderstorms developed during the early morning hours of August 1st near Panama City, resulting in flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding was reported along Thomas Drive in Panama City Beach with running water reducing it to one lane.  Dual-pol radar rainfall estimates ranged from 5.50-6.25 in about a 3 hour period.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep moisture and light east flow led to sea breeze storms developing across the area.  One of the storms produced a waterspout over the Tampa Bay that briefly came onshore as a tornado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A nearly stationary waterspout developed over the waters of Safety Harbor and was pushed onshore near the 400 block of East Shore Drive in Oldsmar.  Pinellas County Sheriff's Office found damage to shingles of a home, knocked down branches, a tree, a mailbox, and snapped a guide wire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A ridge axis north of Florida brought southeast flow to the area with deep moisture and sea breeze convergence over the western half of the Florida peninsula. Afternoon and early evening thunderstorms produced damaging wind gusts in the Tampa Bay area and southern Nature Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A Twitter user reported awning and sheet metal peeled back on the roof of an addition to a manufactured home in Gibsonton.  Time was estimated from radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A sea breeze front moved inland and produced gusty onshore flow as the tide was going out. This setup is favorable for rip currents in west central Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 14-year-old Polk County boy swimming with his younger brother off the beach at the northern tip of Anna Maria Island disappeared Saturday afternoon, swept away by a rip current.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Light ESE steering flow combined with high moisture content and an unstable airmass produced slow moving afternoon thunderstorms that produced locally heavy rainfall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Light ESE steering flow combined with high moisture content and an unstable airmass produced slow moving afternoon thunderstorms that produced locally heavy rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall of 2.25-2.49 inches was measured in 90 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface low and cold front tracked over the area from the west. A moist and unstable airmass combined with passing surface and upper level dynamics created locally heavy rainfall and a strong storm in Marion County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter measured 1.57 inches in a half an hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface low and cold front tracked over the area from the west. A moist and unstable airmass combined with passing surface and upper level dynamics created locally heavy rainfall and a strong storm in Marion County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees, power lines, and a barn roof were damaged from thunderstorms winds. The cost of damage was estimated for inclusion of the event in Storm Data.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface low and cold front tracked over the area from the west. A moist and unstable airmass combined with passing surface and upper level dynamics created locally heavy rainfall and a strong storm in Marion County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local WOKV Radio reported flooding in areas of Jacksonville Beach.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of low pressure offshore of the NE Florida coast and passing upper level energy triggered a band of coastal showers along the Florida coast...specifically St. Augustine Shores.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The public measured 1.24 inches within an hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of low pressure persisted offshore of the NE Florida coast which focused an extended period of onshore flow and caused coastal flooding along the Duval County coast.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike from a thunderstorm centered about 6 to 8 miles north of Key West caused injury to a police officer.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A police officer was injured when his motorcycle was struck, possibly indirectly, by lightning.  It was unknown whether the original lightning strike was to a utility pole or directly to the right-hand handlebar of the motorcycle.  The officer was waiting at a traffic signal at Palm Avenue and North Roosevelt Boulevard while returning to the Key West Police Station in order to switch to his assigned car after hearing thunder.  The officer experienced a significant jolt which threw his right arm backward, causing a shoulder injury.  The officer remained conscious and was able to call for rescue service using his own radio.   The officer was admitted for overnight observation and medical testing and did not receive any burns from the lightning strike.  The officer was released shortly thereafter and was expected to return to duty soon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of showers and thunderstorms moved inland from the Gulf of Mexico during the afternoon and evening hours. A meso low formed within one storm with a strong hook echo signature and velocity signature noted over western Marion county, just west of Ocala and NW of the Ocala Regional Airport during the afternoon. The low level rotation weakened within a 15 minutes, but a tornado warning was issued as the strong was tracking eastward along a small scale warm front and continued to show very favorable radar signatures of rotation. The parent storm did some wind damage, but it eventually morph into a mesolow feature, with a trailing WSW squall line of storms that produced additional wind and hail damage just south of the rotating meso low over southern Marion county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An observer measured 2 inches of rainfall within 50 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mean layer trough axis extended over the forecast area...with the Atlantic surface ridge axis over south Florida. Convection fired along the trough axis over the coastal waters and over coastal counties with locally heavy rainfall and gusty winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A short wave trough moved over Florida during the afternoon and evening which provided additional destabilization and moisture to support numerous strong to severe afternoon and evening showers and thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees were blown down on the eastbound on-ramp of Interstate 10, exit 333.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees were blown down on Highway 17 at Wells Road.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was blown down and blocking the road near 395 Dunwoodie Road.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was blown down across Tolar White Road in the Greensboro area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong south winds aided in the development of strong rip currents on the afternoon of Saturday July 20 in the surf zone along the beaches of NW Florida from Pensacola Beach to Destin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two swimmers in the surf were overtaken by strong rip currents.  One of the swimmers was rescued by lifeguards and taken to the hospital where he recovered from his injuries, while the other drowned and was found 2 hours later just before sunset.  The drowning victim was Jeremy Aaron Johnson, a 39 year old physical fitness trainer from New Orleans, Louisiana who was vacationing in the Navarre area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A collision of three boundaries including the east coast sea breeze moving inland, and two outflow boundaries, one from the south and one from the west, converge under and intensifying thunderstorm over the Fort Caroline area. Rapid convergence and uplift spawned a brief tornado which caused structural damage to an apartment complex and widespread tree and some roof damage to homes in the area. One injury occurred. The slow storm motion also produced significant rainfall amounts, with caused flooding from Arlington toward the Mayport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Neighborhoods started to have flooded roadways along Atlantic Blvd, east of Interstate 95 around 4:30 pm local time. Also, an off duty NWS employee reported 3 inches of water near the Town and Country Shopping Center in Arlington. By 4:45 pm, knee deep water was reported at the Singleton's Seafood Restaurant near Mayport. There was an estimated 3 inches of rainfall within 15 minutes (4:15pm-4:30 pm). By 5 pm, the public relayed a report that the intersection of Beach Blvd and University Blvd South was inundated with water. The cost of damage was estimated for inclusion of the event in Storm Data, and it is likely grossly underestimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: TS Dorian was offshore of the NE Florida Atlantic Coast and tracked NE during the day. Scattered afternoon and evening showers and thunderstorms developed along the sea breezes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was observed about 3 miles SW of Fernandina Beach over the Intracoastal Waterway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface front lifted northward over south Georgia with weaker SW steering flow in place. Diurnal heating combined with sea breeze and outflow activity spawned a few severe storms over the area during the evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Ocala Star Banner reported that lightning struck a home which blasted a large hole in the roof and caused a fire in the attic of the home. The time of damage was based on radar and lightning data. The cost of damage is likely  not accurate and was estimated for the event to be included in Storm Data.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface front lifted northward over south Georgia with weaker SW steering flow in place. Diurnal heating combined with sea breeze and outflow activity spawned a few severe storms over the area during the evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was blown down and blocked the roadway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level area of low pressure over the eastern Gulf of Mexico caused numerous showers and thunderstorms across the southeast Florida coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two 16-year old males fell from a 25-foot Ficus Tree that was struck by lightning and were injured. Both were transported to a local hospital.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak tropical wave over Cuba produced scattered afternoon showers and thunderstorms across South Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An off duty NWS employee reported a funnel cloud east-southeast of Kendall Regional Medical Center.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tropical wave along with an upper level low pressure area near Nassau produced scattered showers and thunderstorms across South Florida and the adjacent waters.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement and the Naples Daily News reported a Pennsylvania woman was injured when a lightning strike occurred near the the Marco Beach Ocean Resort at 480 S. Collier Blvd. She was taken to a local hospital and treated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist and unstable environment led to scattered afternoon showers and thunderstorms across portions of South Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter reported two successive funnel clouds extending nearly halfway to the ground just east of Belle Glade.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level low pressure area over South Florida produced scattered afternoon showers and thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was reported near the intersection of Indiantown Road and west of the Florida Turnpike.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The northern extent of a tropical wave over the Bahamas helped to trigger afternoon and evening thunderstorms across south Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A boy who was trying to seek shelter from a thunderstorm underneath a chickee hut at Marriott Beach Resort was injured when a lightning struck the hut. He was taken to a local hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. The lightning strike also set the chickee hut on fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A microburst produced winds over 60 mph and affected traffic on State Road 528 in rural, eastern Orange County. Tree damage also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A southward moving thunderstorm became severe and produced damaging microburst winds, estimated near 65 mph, impacting State Road 528, just west of the intersection with State Road 520 in rural, southeastern Orange County. Motorists driving eastbound on State Road 528 experienced sudden gusts of swirling winds along the leading edge of the microburst, which lofted dirt and small debris tens of feet into the air. Numerous branches were downed in the area and at least one tree was toppled and fell onto State Road 528.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A microburst produced winds over 60 mph and affected traffic on State Road 528 in rural, eastern Orange County. Tree damage also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Orlando media relayed a public report of nickel sized hail falling in Pine Castle as a strong thunderstorm impacted the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong system affected the area at the end of January with unusually strong wind fields.  The 500 mb winds were as high as 110 knots with the upper level trough, and 850 mb winds reached around 70 knots in the local area. These very strong low level winds combined with SBCAPE values of 300-500 j/kg produced widespread straight line wind damage across the area with numerous trees and power lines blown down as a squall line moved through the area.  Strong non-convective related winds also occurred ahead of the main line of convection and resulted in a few trees being blown down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees were blown down across Walton county.  Monetary damage was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A broken line of showers and thunderstorms moved rapidly southeast across Volusia County. Two cells along the line caused minor wind damage to trees, but no structural damage. Part of a tree fell onto powerlines and a vehicle in Deltona, and tree limbs temporarily blocked a road in Osteen.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds, estimated as sub-severe by fire department officials at the damage scene, downed an old tree, damaging a car, powerlines and a fence in Deltona. Nearby in Osteen, tree limbs were downed by the strong winds at the same time,  resulting in a briefly closure of the roadway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep layer cyclonic flow and very high moisture content produced several days of soaking rainfall during a develop local Nor'Easter event. After locally heavy rainfall on May 2 and 3, strong onshore flow and gusty winds of 40 to 50 mph started to caused trees to come down over inland northeast Florida from Bradford county to Flagler county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A branch of the Cedar River was escaping its banks and causing areal flooding. The cost of damage was unknown, and only estimated so the event could be included in Storm Data.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep layer cyclonic flow and very high moisture content produced several days of soaking rainfall during a develop local Nor'Easter event. After locally heavy rainfall on May 2 and 3, strong onshore flow and gusty winds of 40 to 50 mph started to caused trees to come down over inland northeast Florida from Bradford county to Flagler county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There were several roads in Penny Farms closed due to standing flood waters due to heavy rainfall. Water was up to the bottom of the bridge on Everett Avenue at Dillaberry Branch which runs into Black Creek. Also. the water was spilling over North Dolphin Avenue. Creeks were coming out of their banks and additional flooding occurred in the Foxmeadow subdivision. A spotter measured a 24 hr rainfall total in Foxmeadow of 4.26 inches. The cost of damage from flooding was estimated only so the event could be included in Storm Data. The value is likely a gross underestimate.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong low level jet of 60-70 kts around 850 mb preceded a squall line as it moved over the forecast area. Instability was negligible, with one a few lightning strikes noted over western counties of SE Ga. Some of the strongest winds and minor wind damage occurred just ahead of the main squall line, which downed some trees and powerlines. Several clusters of convection became severe and produced more widespread tree damage...confined to the Suwannee River Valley and over SE Ga.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees were blown down in the Suwannee River State Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface low deepened and tracked northeastward through the Florida Panhandle and southern Georgia during the morning hours. Ahead of this low, a warm front was able to move northward and bring an unstable airmass into portions of the Florida Panhandle. Strong shear in place aided in the development of isolated severe thunderstorms with a couple of weak tornadoes and straight line wind damage reports.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A storm chaser reported a funnel cloud crossing over Highway 20 in Calhoun county.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong NE gradient winds trailing a frontal passage caused damage across the area with sustained speeds of 30-40 mph with gusts of 45-50 mph. Winds progressively decreased during the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree fell onto a powerline in Palm Coast off of Flamingo Drive. The cost of damage was estimated so the event could be included in StormData, and the cost is likely an underestimate.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong NE gradient winds trailing a frontal passage caused damage across the area with sustained speeds of 30-40 mph with gusts of 45-50 mph. Winds progressively decreased during the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power lines were blown down along South Central Avenue. The cost of damage was estimated so the event could be included in StormData.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong NE gradient winds trailing a frontal passage caused damage across the area with sustained speeds of 30-40 mph with gusts of 45-50 mph. Winds progressively decreased during the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power lines were blown down in Flagler Beach near North 14th and Central Ave. Strong NE winds were sustained around 25-30 mph with gusts of 35-40 mph along the coast around the time of the event. The cost of damage was estimated so the event could  be included in StormData.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface low deepened and tracked northeastward through the Florida Panhandle and southern Georgia during the morning hours. Ahead of this low, a warm front was able to move northward and bring an unstable airmass into portions of the Florida Panhandle. Strong shear in place aided in the development of isolated severe thunderstorms with a couple of weak tornadoes and straight line wind damage reports.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There were reports of a tornado touchdown with trees snapped or uprooted in the Red Hill area.  This tornado was rated EF0 with max winds around 75 mph and a path length of 4.23 miles.  This tornado was spawned from the same supercell that spawned the Gulf county tornado, but this appears to have been a separate tornado.  This is a very rural area and no structures were hit.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong local Nor'Easter conditions persisted for a couple of days which brought minor coastal flooding and beach erosion around the times of high tide.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The NOS tidal station at Mayport measured an elevated water level of 6.52 ft relative to MLLW which was 0.56 ft above the predicted level. Minor coastal flooding typically begins at 6.4 ft MLLW.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The NOS station at Mayport measured 6.37 ft relative to MLLW which was 0.43 ft above predicted levels.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The NOS tide gauge Fernandina Beach measured a water level of 8.04 ft relative to MLLW which was 0.28 ft above the predicted level.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong NE gradient winds trailing a frontal passage caused damage across the area with sustained speeds of 30-40 mph with gusts of 45-50 mph. Winds progressively decreased during the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree fell through a roof of a home in the 5100 Block of Big Oak Rd South. There were 4 adults and 1 child inside the home at the time, but no injuries occurred. The residents were relocated. The time of damage was based on observations from the St. Augustine record. The cost of damage was estimated so the event could be logged into StormData, and the estimate is likely grossly underestimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of weak surface low pressure systems draped across the the deep south including the central Florida peninsula combined with an approaching cold front over a very saturated airmass triggered a cluster of storms over Nassau county that produced a strong outflow that tracked SE over western Duval county. When this outflowed encountered ESE flow and feeder bands moving inland from the Atlantic, the cell became nearly stationary over southern Duval and NE Clay counties where it produced flash flooding during the evening. The cell pulsed up and became severe over northern St. Johns county where low level helicity was greatly increased from the strong 20 kt surface winds from the ESE on the north side of a meso low that lifted northward over Alachua and Putnam counties. The cell began to strong very strong and deep layer rotation over northern St. Johns county, and a tornado warning was issued. The cell then collapsed within the next few radar scans, but weak rotation was still present. When a very strong downburst raced southward over St. Johns county, the downburst produced widespread damage to farmland near Elkton and a weak EF1 tornado touched down, on the bookend of the downburst. Flooding rainfall and deep hail drifts of penny size hail caused extensive damage to cropland near Elkton, with severe financial impact to farmers in the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minor flooding was reported at Happy Bluff and Forest Circle. Water was backing up through storm drains due to elevated water levels in the St. Johns River. The cost of flooding damage was estimated.

A piece of energy traveled over the area during peak heating and this instability and forcing combined with adequate mean layer moisture and forcing from the sea breezes spawned scattered afternoon and evening severe storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pea to penny size hail was reported in Fort Mccoy.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorms produced wind damage in the Florida panhandle.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A downed tree knocked down several utility lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of weak surface low pressure systems draped across the the deep south including the central Florida peninsula combined with an approaching cold front over a very saturated airmass triggered a cluster of storms over Nassau county that produced a strong outflow that tracked SE over western Duval county. When this outflowed encountered ESE flow and feeder bands moving inland from the Atlantic, the cell became nearly stationary over southern Duval and NE Clay counties where it produced flash flooding during the evening. The cell pulsed up and became severe over northern St. Johns county where low level helicity was greatly increased from the strong 20 kt surface winds from the ESE on the north side of a meso low that lifted northward over Alachua and Putnam counties. The cell began to strong very strong and deep layer rotation over northern St. Johns county, and a tornado warning was issued. The cell then collapsed within the next few radar scans, but weak rotation was still present. When a very strong downburst raced southward over St. Johns county, the downburst produced widespread damage to farmland near Elkton and a weak EF1 tornado touched down, on the bookend of the downburst. Flooding rainfall and deep hail drifts of penny size hail caused extensive damage to cropland near Elkton, with severe financial impact to farmers in the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Street flooding occurred along State Road 16 and State Road 13. Widespread water over the roadways was reported. The public reported flooding at Lake Julia in the vicinity of Sawgrass County Club in Pone Vedra. Heavy rainfall combined with drifts of hail caused major crop damage near Hastings and Elkton. Potato plants were severely damaged, and about 60% of the St. Johns County potato crop was likely lost. The cost of potato crop damage to about 8,500 acres of plants was estimated to be around $38,250,000, based on a yield of $4.5K/acre of potatoes. Melons and field corn crops were also damaged. The County Commission voted to pursue federal disaster relief funding. The crops were maintained through late season freezes in February and March, so many farmers were trying to rebound from these expensive events. The damage from the heavy rainfall and hail was very devastating to farmers and suppliers.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of an approaching but weakening short wave trough over SW Georgia, convective outflow boundaries and the sea breezes over a moist and unstable airmass triggered scattered strong to severe storms over NE Florida during the late afternoon and evening hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A large tree was blown down which knocked down power lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High winds from a thunderstorms downed trees in the western Florida panhandle.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds estimated a 60 mph downed two trees on Antioch Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep layer cyclonic flow and very high moisture content produced several days of soaking rainfall during a develop local Nor'Easter event. After locally heavy rainfall on May 2 and 3, strong onshore flow and gusty winds of 40 to 50 mph started to caused trees to come down over inland northeast Florida from Bradford county to Flagler county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding occurred in downtown St. Augustine near King and Cordova Streets, and the surrounding areas. Vehicles were trying to escape the area due to rising high water. The cost of damage in the area was estimated for the event to be included in Storm Data. Note that the true cost of damage was unknown.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface low was lifting over north central Florida. Strong upper level winds created enhanced divergence over the area and combined with large lift in the hail growth zone, early morning supercells produced large hail over SE Ga. As the day progressed, upper level forcing converged with the warm front over NE FL to produce a long-lived supercell storm that track along and just south of the Interstate 10 corridor from late morning through the mid afternoon hours. This cell produced widespread wind damage and large hail throughout its track.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Baseball size hail was reported 3 miles west of Lawtey near State Road 225. The time of the report was based on radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep layer shear, steep mid-level lapse rates, and sufficient instability came together to produce several severe thunderstorms across the area.  Large hail was the primary threat, although some isolated occurrences of wind damage also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickel size hail was reported via WJHG's facebook page.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm front moved north of the region as a strong upper level trough and surface cold front approached from the west.  The trough took on a negative tilt as it lifted just north of the area and the cold front moved through.  There was sufficient instability and shear for a few strong to severe thunderstorms across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The roof of a workshop was peeled back due to thunderstorm winds.  Monetary damage was estimated.

Deep moisture and southeast winds fueled scattered afternoon thunderstorms over central and southwest Florida that moved northwest towards the coast.  Lightning from one of the storms struck and killed a woman walking on Belleair Beach.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pinellas County emergency management and print and broadcast media reported a 51 year old woman was struck and killed while walking on Belleair Beach.  Print media reported she was walking on the beach with a friend.  When storms started to roll in, the two friends got separated.  After the storms cleared, the friend found the woman's body on the beach. The death was called into the Sheriff's Department at 6:47 PM EDT, so an estimated time of 6:45 PM EDT was used.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold late-season air mass settled over South Florida, causing temperatures to drop to near or slightly below freezing on the morning of March 4th across the Lake Okechobee and interior areas of southern Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Temperatures in the low to mid 30s in western Palm Beach County led to frost formation during the early morning hours of March 4th, leading to some crop damage. Damage was mainly to corn, with less than 20% of the crop damaged. Crop damage is estimated and based on total number of acres damaged which was approximately 3,000-4,000 acres. ||Unofficial temperature readings in some of the fields were as low as the mid 20s, but these values were likely not representative of the larger area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal squall line of storms moved across northeast Florida through the day. Strong low level jet winds combined with sfc based instability and dry air entrainment supported a few severe storms over the area during the morning hours. By the afternoon as the line pushed southeast of northeast Florida...drier air limited convective formation and the severe storm threat greatly decreased.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds downed a tree south of Jennings along Highway 17.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface low was lifting over north central Florida. Strong upper level winds created enhanced divergence over the area and combined with large lift in the hail growth zone, early morning supercells produced large hail over SE Ga. As the day progressed, upper level forcing converged with the warm front over NE FL to produce a long-lived supercell storm that track along and just south of the Interstate 10 corridor from late morning through the mid afternoon hours. This cell produced widespread wind damage and large hail throughout its track.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was observed near Lawtey.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved into South Florida and stalled resulting in isolated strong to severe thunderstorms over metropolitan Broward and Palm Beach Counties during the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter near the intersection of I-95 and I-595 reported a funnel cloud to the southeast. Movement was to the east.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees were blown down at SE County Road 2082 and County Road 234. One tree hit a bridge on County Road 234 and the road was closed until the bridge was inspected and considered safe for travel. The cost of damage was estimated for event inclusion in Storm Data.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A controlled burn for originally 70 acres transformed into a wildfire due to varying wind shifts near the marshlands. The resultant wildfire burned about 400 acres. Two homes were in danger, but fire fighters were able to save the structures. Roads were closed in the area including Black Hammock Island was shut down to traffic. Other roads west of the island that were closed included Nungezer Road, Cedar Point Drive and Boney Road.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Pumpkin Hill Preserve wildfire started as a controlled burn. Dry fuels combined with breezy and variable winds given the locations close proximity to the coast and nearby estuaries enabled the fire to quickly grow to a wildfire by noon on the 2nd. Overnight on the 3rd crews were able to control the fire. The fire burned 400 acres. No structures were damaged.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: As a cold front moved south into the region and stalled, a series of upper level disturbances moved through the area with broad southwest flow and moist conditions.  As the boundary became oriented parallel to the upper level winds, training bands of convection set up and produced some locally heavy rainfall, although flash flooding was limited to adjacent sections of southeastern Alabama.  A few strong to severe storms also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: All dirt roads between Highway 79 and Highway 177A near the Wrights Creek area were impassable due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wildfire started in the Halfmoon Lake area from an illegal camp fire. The fire burned a total of 150 acres before it was contained.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Halfmoon Lake wildfire started on April 7th and continued into the 8th. The fire started south of State Road 40 about 5 miles WSW of Juniper Springs. The fire burned about 400 acres. Firelines were created around 200 homes in the Lakes in the Forest neighborhood.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees and power lines were blown down at Birch Street and Lakeview Trail.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Charlotte County Sheriffs Office relayed a report of 2 downed banyan trees blocking Green Dolphin Drive in Placida.  Time estimated based on radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep southwesterly flow led to the development of several strong to severe thunderstorms, which moved from the Orlando area to the coast of Volusia and Brevard Counties. Peak winds gusts reached 66 mph in Orlando. Thunderstorm winds also peeled back part of a roof from a home in Port Orange.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Orlando Executive Airport ASOS (KORL) measured a wind gust of 66 mph from the southwest as a thunderstorm rapidly, briefly, and locally became severe.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of thunderstorms moved east across Seminole and Orange Counties. One storm within the line became severe and produced a microburst with peak winds of 76 mph measured at the Orlando-Sanford Airport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ASOS at Sanford-Orlando Airport (KSFB) measured peak winds from the south of 76 mph as a thunderstorm produced a severe microburst at the airport. Tower personnel reported a loaded luggage cart was blown over onto an active taxiway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Southwest flow aloft pushed several showers and thunderstorms off the coast of Brevard County. Trident Pier at Port Canaveral measured a wind gust of 51 knots as the storm moved over the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A C-MAN station at Trident Pier reported a 51 knot gusts as a severe thunderstorm moved from the barrier island to the adjacent Atlantic.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low pressure area over the northeast Gulf of Mexico and a surface trough across central Florida combined to produce numerous strong to severe thunderstorms over portions of southeast Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Extensive street flooding was reported in Delray Beach since early afternoon with the area affected bounded by I-95 and Jog Road to the east and west, and roughly from Linton Boulevard and Atlantic Avenue to the north and south. Kings Point neighborhood was told to remain inside until the following morning. Delray Beach Medical Center reported minor flooding on the first floor with measured rainfall of 8.47 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low pressure area over the northeast Gulf of Mexico and a surface trough across central Florida combined to produce numerous strong to severe thunderstorms over portions of southeast Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An EF0 tornado touched down near North Dixie Highway and NE 28th St. in northeastern sections of Boca Raton and traveled to near Federal Highway and NE 32nd St. Maximum winds were estimated at 70 mph with damage consisting primarily of downed trees, fences and power lines, with a piece of metal-covered foam roof blown through a house window and a damaged store sign.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level trough of low pressure moved across South Florida producing scattered strong to severe thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A report of penny-sized hail was received from WPBF in Palm Beach County 1 mile SSE of Jupiter Farms.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure over the southeastern states resulted in breezy onshore winds along the southeast coast of the Florida peninsula with strong rip currents.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A fifteen-year-old boy drowned at John U. Lloyd State Park due to rip currents. The beach was unguarded but red rip current warning flags were posted at the entrance to the park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface trough of low pressure was located near Lake Okeechobee resulting in an isolated severe thunderstorm in Glades County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local law enforcement reported four of five downed trees in the vicinity of Buckhead Ridge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface trough of low pressure located across South Florida resulted in scattered late afternoon thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A report was received of penny-sized hail 2 miles north-northeast of the Sawgrass Mills Mall. The report was relayed by Channel 7 news.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface trough of low pressure located across South Florida resulted in scattered late afternoon thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A meteorologist with WSVN witnessed nickel-sized hail at the Sawgrass Mills Mall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface trough of low pressure located across South Florida resulted in scattered late afternoon thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Twitter report received of strong winds causing tree damage in Ave Maria. A 20-foot tree was split in half and a few smaller trees were uprooted.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure over the southeastern states resulted in breezy onshore winds along the southeast coast of the Florida peninsula with strong rip currents. At least three-dozen swimmers were rescued from rip currents.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong rip currents along the Palm Beach County beaches led to at least 11 rescues. Three juveniles required hospitalization after being rescued near Lake Worth Pier.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid to upper level low pressure area over Florida combined with a moist east flow to produce periods of torrential downpours and thunderstorms over the course of the day across southeast Florida. Over 2,000 customers were without power in the three-county region of southeast Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains and flooding affected portions of western Boynton Beach and Aberdeen. Rainfall totals were estimated in the 4 to 9 inch range, with measured amounts nearby of up to 8 inches in less than 12 hours. Impacts were primarily to closed streets, fields and parking lots. Park Vista High School was in lock-down mode during the midday hour due to the extent and magnitude of the flooding surrounding the school.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep tropical moisture persisted across South Florida along with onshore flow along the southeast coast as high pressure persisted over the southeast states. This resulted in a cluster of heavy showers that moved from the Atlantic into northeast Palm Beach County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A cluster of very heavy showers moved into northeast Palm Beach County during the early morning producing rainfall estimates of 4 to 7 inches in the areas from The Acreage to Boynton Beach. Flood waters was reported to be entering garages in The Acreage and Park Vista High School in Boynton Beach was reported closed due to flooded parking lots.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid to upper level low pressure area over Florida combined with a moist east flow to produce periods of torrential downpours and thunderstorms over the course of the day across southeast Florida. Over 2,000 customers were without power in the three-county region of southeast Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Palm Beach County Sheriff's Office reported severe road flooding in the Riviera Beach area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm formed over southern Brevard County as an outflow boundary merged with the east coast sea breeze that was pinned close to the coast. Palm Bay Police Department relayed a report that trees fell onto live wires in Palm Bay.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Palm Bay Police Department reported trees down onto live wires near the intersection of Pace Drive NW and Lamplight Drive NW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid to upper level low pressure area over Florida combined with a moist east flow to produce periods of torrential downpours and thunderstorms over the course of the day across southeast Florida. Over 2,000 customers were without power in the three-county region of southeast Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two persons, a 40-year-old man and a 32-year-old woman, were struck by lightning at Aventura Mall close to 10 PM EDT. Both were hospitalized and were released the following day.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High moisture and instability led to thunderstorms developing over parts of southeast Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck the roof of a home in Coral Springs. Tiles were removed from the roof, with no other damage or injuries. Time estimated based on lightning archive data.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High moisture and instability led to thunderstorms developing over parts of southeast Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pictures were relayed by WSVN-TV of funnel clouds in the Princeton area of southern Miami-Dade County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure persisted over the southeast states resulting in an onshore wind along the southeast Florida coast and strong rip currents.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 17-year-old young man went missing at the Gulf Stream Park Beach in Boynton Beach, presumed to be swept away by a rip current. Rescue efforts both Friday afternoon and Saturday were unsuccessful and the Coast Guard suspended its search efforts a few days later.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep southwesterly flow led to the development of several strong to severe thunderstorms, which moved from the Orlando area to the coast of Volusia and Brevard Counties. Peak winds gusts reached 66 mph in Orlando. Thunderstorm winds also peeled back part of a roof from a home in Port Orange.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced winds which peeled back the roof from an enclosed porch of a mobile home in Port Orange.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low moving across the eastern Gulf of Mexico provided increased instability for afternoon showers and thunderstorms.  Several of these storms caused damaging wind gusts.  Additionally, a couple lightning strikes caused fires.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Florida State watch office reported a lightning strike to an apartment complex started a fire near 4th Street North and 112 Avenue Northeast.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low moving across the eastern Gulf of Mexico provided increased instability for afternoon showers and thunderstorms.  Several of these storms caused damaging wind gusts.  Additionally, a couple lightning strikes caused fires.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media reported that a thunderstorm wind gust tore the roof off of a mobile home which landed on another nearby mobile home in Villa Del Sol in Bradenton.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous showers and thunderstorms occurred during July 21-23 with some storms becoming severe and knocking several trees down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree and a power line were blown down near Bruner Dairy Road in Vernon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very anomalous pattern set up across the U.S. in early July featuring a persistent upper level trough over and just west of the Mississippi Valley and a downstream ridge off the Mid Atlantic Coast. This brought deep layer moist tropical air into the Southeast for several consecutive days resulting in torrential rains, flash flooding and river flooding. While several episodes of severe weather also occurred during this period, the heavy rain and accompanying flooding were the primary impacts.  Between 10 and 20 inches of rain fell in a three day period between July 2-4, and serious flooding occurred across portions of the Florida Panhandle with Walton, Holmes, and Washington counties declared federal disaster areas.  Damage estimates include $11 million for Walton county, $18.7 million for Washington County (including $3.7 million for the town of Vernon alone), $8.5 million for Holmes county, and $1 million for Bay county.  Most of the damage was due to roads and bridges, but some structures were also affected.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The City of Tallahassee Twitter feed reported 17 trees down in Tallahassee.  Monetary damage was estimated.

This brought deep layer moist tropical air into the Southeast for several consecutive days resulting in torrential rains, flash flooding and river flooding. While several episodes of severe weather also occurred during this period, the heavy rain and accompanying flooding were the primary impacts.  Between 10 and 20 inches of rain fell in a three day period between July 2-4, and serious flooding occurred across portions of the Florida Panhandle with Walton, Holmes, and Washington counties declared federal disaster areas.  Damage estimates include $11 million for Walton county, $18.7 million for Washington County (including $3.7 million for the town of Vernon alone), $8.5 million for Holmes county, and $1 million for Bay county.  Most of the damage was due to roads and bridges, but some structures were also affected.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was blown down and blocking Bayshore Dr.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Easterly winds and deep moisture allowed for a sea breeze collision just along the west coast of Florida, producing scattered to numerous thunderstorms. Some of the storms produced damaging thunderstorm wind gusts, lightning strikes, and heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Largo Police Department reported Belchar Road was closed from Ulmerton Road to 142nd Avenue with an estimated 2 feet of water on the road.  Additionally, an estimated 3 feet of water covered Floral Drive and 36th Street Southeast.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was reported just off the coast west of Naples. Also reported via social media.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of high pressure located across the Atlantic resulted in onshore winds along the southeast coast of Florida and strong rip currents at the beaches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media and a local newspaper reported several rip current rescues and one drowning along the coast of Broward County during the afternoon. Fort Lauderdale Fire Rescue responded to a husband and wife getting caught in a strong rip current at an unguarded beach at 4200 Galt Ocean Drive at about 1:30 PM EDT. They were transported to Holy Cross Hospital with life threatening injuries where the 80-year old woman was responsive but her 83-year old husband was not. He was later pronounced dead. Two men and a woman, ages unknown, were rescued from a rip current just north of Hallandale Beach. The men were transported to Aventura Hospital but the woman refused treatment.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of high pressure located across the Atlantic resulted in onshore winds along the southeast coast of Florida and strong rip currents at the beaches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A man, unknown age, nearly drowned at Ocean Reef Park in Riviera Beach and was taken to St. Mary's Medical Center late Thursday with injuries that were considered life-threatening.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist and unstable airmass was over South Florida which produced scattered showers and thunderstorms during the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 42-year old male crane operator was injured when his crane was struck by lightning in Lauderhill. He was taken to Broward Health Medical Center where he was listed in fair condition.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist and unstable airmass was over South Florida which produced scattered showers and thunderstorms during the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast Media and Law Enforcement reported a weak tornado touched down at Power Catamarans of South Florida damaging dinghies and watercraft. A couple of boats were overturned in the marina with one boat having a bent up shade and a catamaran was pushed into a fence. The force of the tornado also picked up three small Livingston catamarans and blew them across the yard along with a couple more on a rack. There was also one downed power line and some flashing was torn from a building.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Santa Rosa Emergency Services reported they had closed portions of Onich Driver, Shipton Street and Ortega Street due to at least 6 inches of water covering the roadways.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An outflow boundary collision produced a thin line of thunderstorms with a weak circulation. The storm generated a funnel cloud that briefly touched ground as a tornado damaging several homes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency management relayed a report of a brief tornado causing damage in a mobile home park in Palmetto near 10th Street SW and 28th Avenue W.  In total, 7 mobile homes sustained damage, 3 of which suffering moderate to heavy damage.  Additionally, one person was treated for minor injuries caused by flying debris. Multiple sources relayed pictures of a funnel cloud from the same storm.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media reported that a thunderstorm wind gust partially peeled the metal roof off of a building.  Nearby trees and other buildings were not impacted.  Time of damage was estimated from radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure over the Atlantic resulted in an onshore flow across southeast Florida with strong rip currents at the beaches during the weekend of July 6 and 7. Dozens of people were rescued at southeast Florida beaches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Palm Beach County Fire Rescue responded to a call around 5:37 PM EDT that as many as six swimmers were caught in a strong rip current. Upon arrival, rescue crews discovered that there were a total of four swimmers and an unknown number of rescuers. The four swimmers had already been rescued by bystanders and left the scene. One adult male, however, had been caught in a rip current helping to rescue young swimmers at Kreusler Park and was transported to JFK Medical Center in critical condition.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure over the Atlantic produced onshore winds along the southeast coast with strong rip currents developing at the beaches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 23-year old male drowned in a strong rip current at Fort Lauderdale Beach trying to save six of his friends when they were caught in the current. It occurred during the early evening after lifeguards had left for the day and the seven friends did not heed the red flag warnings lining the beaches. Lifeguards had saved 41 people on Sunday in the same location.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous showers and thunderstorms occurred during July 21-23 with some storms becoming severe and knocking several trees down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A large oak tree was blown down across Old St. Augustine Road near Verdura Way.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level area of low pressure over the eastern Gulf of Mexico caused numerous showers and thunderstorms across the southeast Florida coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An intense thunderstorm moved across the southern portion of Miami Beach during the late morning and midday hours, with two to four inches of rain falling in a very short time. Numerous reports of flooding was obtained from Broadcast and Social Media from the Miami Beach area at noon into the early afternoon, particularly south of 41st Street. Water was entering several structures and significant street flooding also occurred. Rain poured in through the roof of the Chabad House which entered the main social area of the center.  A second storm moved across the same area later in the afternoon bringing the rainfall total for the day at Miami Beach of 6.78 inches which not only was a daily record but was a daily record for the month of July and this total made it the wettest July on record with continuous records dating back to 1942.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low pressure area over the northern Gulf of Mexico continued a moist flow of tropical air across South Florida with isolated showers and thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A waterspout moved onshore during a lifeguard competition near the Pompano Beach Fishing Pier. Tents were blown down and there were three minor injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface front lifted northward over south Georgia with weaker SW steering flow in place. Diurnal heating combined with sea breeze and outflow activity spawned a few severe storms over the area during the evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Ocala Star Banner reported that the Marion County Sheriff received multiple reports of downed trees and power lines within the Ocala city limits. A car was struck by a tree near the intersection of SW 80th Street and 19th Avenue. The time of the damage was based on radar imagery.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wet month led to some minor flooding along the Steinhatchee River in southeast Taylor county.  A house was also struck by lightning in Panama City Beach.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The media reported a house fire started by lightning near the intersection of Hutchison Blvd and Azalea St in Panama City Beach.  Monetary damage was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A ridge axis north of Florida brought southeast flow to the area with deep moisture and sea breeze convergence over the western half of the Florida peninsula. Afternoon and early evening thunderstorms produced damaging wind gusts in the Tampa Bay area and southern Nature Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pasco county emergency management relayed reports of at least two downed trees in the Moon Lake area.  Time estimated from radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous showers and thunderstorms occurred during July 21-23 with some storms becoming severe and knocking several trees down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was blown down on Highway 221 just north of Greenville with no other damage reported in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cluster of thunderstorms over the Everglades merged with the sea breeze in the Naples area and led to numerous thunderstorms with heavy rain in the evening hours. In addition to the lightning impacts, a total of 6-10 inches of rain fell in the North Naples area between 8 pm and midnight. This caused significant street flooding in the Quail Run community where about a dozen cars were stranded and had to be towed away, as well as forcing the closure of several area streets.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck the exterior wall of an apartment building in East Naples, causing a fire which affected mainly the attic with moderate damage. Crews responded to the fire the following morning. Time of lightning strike estimated based on archived radar and lightning data.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tup McWaters Road was closed at the west end at the bridge due to flash flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The northern extent of a weak tropical wave over the northwest Bahamas produced breezy onshore winds along the southeast Florida coast with dangerous rip currents at the beaches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong rip currents occurred during the day along the Atlantic beaches with about 50 rescues reported by Miami Beach Ocean Rescue. An elderly man and woman were pulled from the water by Ocean Rescue lifeguards at Allison Park near 64th Street and Collins Avenue. They were rushed to Mt. Sinai Medical Center but doctors were unable to save them.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 17-year old male was found under water at Dubois Park in Jupiter after being caught in a rip current. He was taken to a local hospital and his condition is not known.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A member of the public reported a funnel cloud near Pelican Lake east of Pahokee.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very anomalous pattern set up across the U.S. in early July featuring a persistent upper level trough over and just west of the Mississippi Valley and a downstream ridge off the Mid Atlantic Coast. This brought deep layer moist tropical air into the Southeast for several consecutive days resulting in torrential rains, flash flooding and river flooding. While several episodes of severe weather also occurred during this period, the heavy rain and accompanying flooding were the primary impacts.  Between 10 and 20 inches of rain fell in a three day period between July 2-4, and serious flooding occurred across portions of the Florida Panhandle with Walton, Holmes, and Washington counties declared federal disaster areas.  Damage estimates include $11 million for Walton county, $18.7 million for Washington County (including $3.7 million for the town of Vernon alone), $8.5 million for Holmes county, and $1 million for Bay county.  Most of the damage was due to roads and bridges, but some structures were also affected.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There were multiple reports of trees down near the city of Madison and in the eastern part of the county.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Sea breeze storms and an unstable airmass triggered evening strong storms with a frontal boundary draped over northeast Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tin roof over a pump house was damaged and small tree limbs were snapped by thunderstorm winds. The cost of damage was estimated for the event to be included in StormData.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of showers and thunderstorms moved inland from the Gulf of Mexico during the afternoon and evening hours. A meso low formed within one storm with a strong hook echo signature and velocity signature noted over western Marion county, just west of Ocala and NW of the Ocala Regional Airport during the afternoon. The low level rotation weakened within a 15 minutes, but a tornado warning was issued as the strong was tracking eastward along a small scale warm front and continued to show very favorable radar signatures of rotation. The parent storm did some wind damage, but it eventually morph into a mesolow feature, with a trailing WSW squall line of storms that produced additional wind and hail damage just south of the rotating meso low over southern Marion county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter estimated wind gusts to 60 mph which damage a tool shed and blew a large oak tree down.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A bolt of lightning struck a van that was being driven southbound on Interstate 95 in Mims. The windshield shattered, the engine was disabled, and all four tires were blown out. The front portion of the van then burst into flames. The driver was able to get out of the vehicle safely, with no injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Localized heavy rainfall associated with an area of slow moving thunderstorms produced between four and seven inches of rain in only a few hours across |western Volusia County. Temporary street flooding, wth water entering one home, were the major impacts. The flooding quickly receded once the heavy rain |ended.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain produced flooding of multiple streets in Deland, with several cars becoming stranded due to high water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed a pre-frontal squall line and developed two prominent bow echoes. The bow echoes raced east at 55-60 mph from the central Florida interior and persisted to the Brevard County coast. Significant straight-line wind damage occurred along two long swaths, from southwest Lake County to Cocoa, and from Orlando to Cape Canaveral. Winds were measured up to 86 mph at two observing sites in Orange County. Much the surrounding areas of Lake, Orange, and Brevard Counties (as well as adjacent portions of Seminole and Osceola Counties) experienced minor wind damage. A woman was injured in Cocoa when a tree fell on the Florida room that she was entering from outdoors. Damage estimates totalled over $3M, of which $2M was from the collapse of a storage warehouse under construction in Orlando. The remainder of the cost estimate was a result of falling trees and mainly minor structural damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two adjacent homes on a large lake experienced major damage to pool screen enclosures from very strong winds which came off the lake. Several trees were also toppled.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of upper level disturbance rotating around a mean longwave trough west of the area brought copious amounts of moisture into the area.  The KTAE rawinsonde sounding on the evening of February 22nd measured a PWAT value of 1.77 inches, which was the 2nd highest PWAT observed during the month of February at that location.  Rainfall amounts generally between 9 and 14 inches were observed during the 5 day period between Feb 22-26 across the Tallahassee area with similar amounts across the remainder of the Florida Panhandle.  This led to areas of flooding.  There were also some severe storms during the early morning hours of Feb 26th.  The southeast big bend saw significantly less rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tornado was spotted crossing over the St. George Island Bridge.  No damage occurred.
